body:
# Security & Edge Cases — Epic

**Spec:** `docs/superpowers/specs/2026-05-24-customer-feedback-round-1-design.md` §18 + §19
**Customer ask:** security review, request limits, edge cases (e.g., admin trying to submit a request and getting an error)
**Milestone:** M7 Security & Edge Cases — no hard due date, runs in parallel with M6, finishes before final demo
**Priority:** P1 — does not block M6 feature work

## Security hardening (§18)
- [ ] #82 — Rate limiting middleware (@mhammadsiag)
- [ ] #83 — Helmet + CORS allowlist (@mhammadsiag)
- [ ] #84 — File upload hardening (@MuhammadMarmash)
- [ ] #85 — Password policy (@mhammadsiag)
- [ ] #86 — Email verification gate (@MuhammadMarmash)
- [ ] #87 — Disabled account handling (@abdullahabulafi)
- [ ] #88 — Open-redirect protection (@hamzakaraky)
- [ ] #89 — Firestore + Storage rules unit-test suite (@abdullahabulafi)

## Edge cases (§19)
- [ ] #90 — Admin-on-/requests UX (@MuhammadMarmash) ← observed at meeting
- [ ] #91 — Deactivated volunteer in-flight assignments (@abdullahabulafi)
- [ ] #92 — Status forward-only race-safe transaction (@abdullahabulafi)
- [ ] #93 — Token expiry mid-form draft restore (@MuhammadMarmash)
- [ ] #94 — Browser back after submit (@MuhammadMarmash)
- [ ] #95 — Assignment language-match warning (@abdullahabulafi)
- [ ] #96 — Filename sanitization (@MuhammadMarmash)

## Owner load (M7)
- @MuhammadMarmash: 5 (#84, #86, #90, #93, #94, #96 = 6)
- @abdullahabulafi: 5 (#87, #89, #91, #92, #95)
- @mhammadsiag: 3 (#82, #83, #85)
- @hamzakaraky: 1 (#88)

## Definition of done
Every checkbox ticked, rules unit-test suite green in CI, and a manual smoke test confirms:
- Rate limiting kicks in.
- Admin cannot submit a request and sees a helpful page.
- Disabled user is signed out cleanly.
- Hebrew filenames upload.
title: FB1-M7: Security & Edge Cases — epic

body:
## Context
Spec: `docs/superpowers/specs/2026-05-24-customer-feedback-round-1-design.md` §19.7. Hebrew/Arabic filenames break Storage paths today.

## Scope
- New util `sanitizeFilename(name): string`:
  - Strips non-ASCII.
  - Replaces spaces with `_`.
  - Lower-cases the extension.
  - Prepends 8-char random hex prefix to avoid collisions.
- Used in the upload helper before constructing `requests/{requestId}/<sanitizedName>`.
- Original display name stored in `requests/{id}.attachments[].displayName` for the UI to show.

## Acceptance criteria
- [ ] \"קורות חיים.pdf\" uploads successfully with a sanitized Storage path.
- [ ] UI shows the original filename in the attachment list.
- [ ] Backend schema accepts the new `attachments[].displayName` field.
title: FB1-M7-E7: Filename sanitization — strip non-ASCII, hash prefix, preserve displayName

body:
## Context
Spec: `docs/superpowers/specs/2026-05-24-customer-feedback-round-1-design.md` §19.6.

## Scope
- Read `users/{beneficiaryId}.preferredLanguage` (default `he` if missing).
- For each volunteer row in the assign modal, compute `languageMatch = volunteer.languages.includes(preferredLanguage)`.
- Show inline \"Language mismatch\" warning chip before the Assign button when `languageMatch === false`.
- Admin can still assign — it's a warning, not a block.

## Acceptance criteria
- [ ] Warning chip appears for mismatched rows only.
- [ ] Chip is keyboard-focusable with a tooltip explaining \"Volunteer doesn't speak X\".
- [ ] HE+EN strings.

## Dependencies
Touches assignment UI from #74.
title: FB1-M7-E6: Assignment modal — warn when volunteer's languages don't include beneficiary's preferred language

body:
## Context
Spec: `docs/superpowers/specs/2026-05-24-customer-feedback-round-1-design.md` §19.5.

## Scope
- After successful submit on `RequestsPage`, navigate via `router.replace('/my-requests?new=<requestId>')` instead of pushing the success screen.
- The success state is rendered from the `?new=` query param on `MyRequestsPage`.
- Pressing browser-back from there goes to the previous page (e.g., homepage), NOT to the form.

## Acceptance criteria
- [ ] Submit → success view → press back → no form reappears.
- [ ] Existing success-screen UI preserved on `MyRequestsPage`.
title: FB1-M7-E5: Browser back after submit — use router.replace() so form doesn't reappear

body:
## Context
Spec: `docs/superpowers/specs/2026-05-24-customer-feedback-round-1-design.md` §19.4.

## Scope
- `RequestsPage` writes a debounced (1s) snapshot to `localStorage.requestDraft.<uid>` on every form change. Cleared on successful submit.
- On submit, if API returns 401:
  - Toast: \"Your session expired. Please sign in again — we saved your form.\"
  - `router.replace('/login?next=/requests')`.
- On `RequestsPage` mount, if a draft exists for the current `uid`, show \"Restore your draft\" banner before clearing.

## Acceptance criteria
- [ ] After idle and 401, user re-logs and recovers all field values.
- [ ] Successful submit removes the draft.
- [ ] HE+EN strings for banner and toast.
title: FB1-M7-E4: Token expiry mid-form — save draft to localStorage, prompt re-login, restore on return

body:
## Context
Spec: `docs/superpowers/specs/2026-05-24-customer-feedback-round-1-design.md` §19.3.

## Scope
- Wrap `POST /api/admin/requests/status` (#75) in a Firestore transaction:
  - Read current status.
  - Validate target is a forward transition: `submitted → assigned → in_progress → resolved/rejected`. Reject backward (`resolved → assigned`) with 400.
  - Write new status + requestEvents entry atomically.
- On concurrent admin clicks: one wins, the other gets 409 `{ error: 'status_already_changed' }` with a friendly toast.

## Acceptance criteria
- [ ] Backward transitions rejected with 400.
- [ ] Concurrent transitions (simulated in test) — only one succeeds.
- [ ] Frontend handles 409 with a refresh prompt.

## Dependencies
Modifies #75.
title: FB1-M7-E3: Request status forward-only — Firestore transaction, race-safe enforcement

body:
## Context
Spec: `docs/superpowers/specs/2026-05-24-customer-feedback-round-1-design.md` §19.2.

## Scope
- Deactivating a volunteer does NOT clear `assignedVolunteerId` on existing requests (we keep history).
- `/admin/requests` list and detail: when `volunteers/{assignedVolunteerId}.active === false`, render `\"שם המתנדב (לשעבר) / Volunteer name (former)\"` muted.
- Admin requests detail surfaces a banner: \"This volunteer has been deactivated. Reassign to keep the request moving.\" with Reassign button reusing the assign modal.

## Acceptance criteria
- [ ] After deactivation, in-flight requests still show the original volunteer's name with \"(former)\" tag.
- [ ] Banner appears only on detail page for requests with deactivated volunteers.
- [ ] Reassign action works end-to-end.

## Dependencies
Touches the same UI as #74 — coordinate.
title: FB1-M7-E2: Deactivated volunteer with in-flight assignments — 'former volunteer' display + reassign banner

body:
## Context
Spec: `docs/superpowers/specs/2026-05-24-customer-feedback-round-1-design.md` §19.1. Customer observed: admin tried to submit a request and got an error.

## Scope
- `RequestsPage` checks `role`. If `admin`, replace page content with:
  - HE/EN card: \"Admins cannot submit requests on this account.\"
  - \"Sign out and continue\" button → signs out, routes to `/login`.
  - \"Back to admin\" link → `/admin`.
- Homepage \"Submit a request\" CTA is hidden when `role === 'admin'`.

## Acceptance criteria
- [ ] Admin navigating to `/requests` sees the card, not a 403 toast.
- [ ] Homepage CTA hidden for admin.
- [ ] HE+EN strings.
title: FB1-M7-E1: Admin-on-/requests UX — show 'switch account' page instead of 403 error

body:
## Context
Spec: `docs/superpowers/specs/2026-05-24-customer-feedback-round-1-design.md` §18.8.

## Scope
- Adopt `@firebase/rules-unit-testing` if not in place.
- Test matrix per collection (existing + new from §12):
  - `requests`, `chats`, `messages`, `auditLogs`, `answers`, `businesses`, `organizations`, `categories`, `regions`, `users`, `volunteerApplications`, `volunteers`, `ratings`, `requestEvents`.
  - Each collection: owner read OK, non-owner read denied, admin read OK, client write denied.
- Storage rules: anonymous upload denied, authed upload accepted within size/MIME limits.

## Acceptance criteria
- [ ] CI runs the suite on every PR.
- [ ] All test cases green against current rules.
- [ ] Any new collection added later must add its test cases in the same PR (document in CONTRIBUTING).
title: FB1-M7-S8: Firestore + Storage rules unit-test suite (@firebase/rules-unit-testing)

body:
## Context
Spec: `docs/superpowers/specs/2026-05-24-customer-feedback-round-1-design.md` §18.7.

## Scope
- New util `validateRedirect(next: string): string | null` — returns `next` only if it matches `^/[A-Za-z0-9_\-./?=&]*$`, otherwise null.
- Apply in `/login` and `/register` before `router.push(next)` and `router.replace(next)`.
- Default to `/` when null.

## Acceptance criteria
- [ ] `/login?next=https://evil.com` redirects to `/` after login, not evil.com.
- [ ] `/login?next=//evil.com` rejected.
- [ ] `/login?next=/requests` works as today.
title: FB1-M7-S7: Open-redirect protection on next= param

body:
## Context
Spec: `docs/superpowers/specs/2026-05-24-customer-feedback-round-1-design.md` §18.6. The §7.5 admin deactivate has no client effect today.

## Scope
- `AuthContext` subscribes to `users/{uid}` doc and reads `.disabled`.
- If `disabled === true`: call `signOut()` and `router.replace('/account-disabled')`.
- New page `/account-disabled` shows generic reason in HE+EN with NGO contact info.

## Acceptance criteria
- [ ] Admin flipping a user's disabled flag while they're signed in logs them out within 5s.
- [ ] Page is reachable when signed out and shows correct copy.
title: FB1-M7-S6: Disabled account handling — AuthContext check + sign-out + reason page

body:
## Context
Spec: `docs/superpowers/specs/2026-05-24-customer-feedback-round-1-design.md` §18.5.

## Scope
- Client: call Firebase Auth `sendEmailVerification` on register (after createUser).
- Backend: middleware `requireVerifiedEmail` on `POST /api/requests` and `POST /api/volunteers/apply`. Returns 403 `{ error: 'email_not_verified' }` if `req.user.email_verified !== true`.
- Frontend banner on `/requests` when unverified: \"Please verify your email\" with \"Resend verification\" button.
- Grandfathering: only fires for accounts created after this lands (check via `createdAt` cutoff in middleware).

## Acceptance criteria
- [ ] New account that hasn't verified email cannot submit a request.
- [ ] Banner with resend button works.
- [ ] Existing accounts unaffected.
- [ ] HE+EN strings.
title: FB1-M7-S5: Email verification gate — require verified email to submit requests

body:
## Context
Spec: `docs/superpowers/specs/2026-05-24-customer-feedback-round-1-design.md` §18.4. Firebase default of 6 chars is weak.

## Scope
- Client: validate on `/register` and any password-change UI. Inline error.
- Server: `POST /api/auth/register` rejects weak passwords if Firebase Identity Platform password policy isn't configured.
- Existing accounts grandfathered — policy applies only to new signups and next change.

## Acceptance criteria
- [ ] `password123` passes; `password` fails; `12345678` fails (no letter is not enforced but document why we picked min digit only).
- [ ] HE+EN error messages clear.
title: FB1-M7-S4: Password policy — min 8 + at least one digit, client + server

body:
## Context
Spec: `docs/superpowers/specs/2026-05-24-customer-feedback-round-1-design.md` §18.3.

## Scope
- Per-file size limit **10 MB**, enforced client-side (block before upload) and via Storage rules.
- MIME allowlist: `image/jpeg`, `image/png`, `image/webp`, `application/pdf`, `application/vnd.openxmlformats-officedocument.wordprocessingml.document`.
- Per-user-per-day quota **50 MB** total across all requests. Track on `users/{uid}.uploadQuotaUsed` + `uploadQuotaResetAt`.
- Storage rules updated: require auth, enforce size + content-type.

## Acceptance criteria
- [ ] Trying to upload an 11 MB file fails client-side with a clear error.
- [ ] Trying to upload a .exe fails (MIME mismatch).
- [ ] Quota exhaustion blocks further uploads until window resets.
- [ ] Storage rules unit test confirms anonymous upload denied.
title: FB1-M7-S3: File upload hardening — size limit, MIME allowlist, per-user quota, Storage rules tighten

body:
## Context
Spec: `docs/superpowers/specs/2026-05-24-customer-feedback-round-1-design.md` §18.2.

## Scope
- `helmet()` middleware with default config (HSTS, X-Content-Type-Options, X-Frame-Options, starter CSP).
- CORS: replace any current `*` wildcard with a strict allowlist driven by `CORS_ALLOWED_ORIGINS` env (comma-separated).
- Dev default: `http://localhost:3000`. Prod: only the deployed frontend origin.

## Acceptance criteria
- [ ] Request from a non-allowed origin is rejected with no CORS headers.
- [ ] Helmet headers visible in response.
- [ ] CSP doesn't break Firebase Auth iframes (test sign-in flow).
title: FB1-M7-S2: Helmet + CORS allowlist (no wildcard in prod) + secure headers

body:
## Context
Spec: `docs/superpowers/specs/2026-05-24-customer-feedback-round-1-design.md` §18.1. Customer ask: \"request limits\".

## Scope
- Install `express-rate-limit`.
- Global middleware: 60 req/min/IP on any authenticated POST/PATCH.
- Per-route overrides:
  - `/api/auth/register`, `/api/volunteers/apply`: 5 req/min/IP.
  - `/api/admin/*`: 120 req/min/IP.
- Exempt `GET` reads.
- 429 response with JSON body `{ error: 'rate_limited', retryAfterSeconds }`.
- Frontend toast on 429: \"Too many requests — please slow down.\"

## Acceptance criteria
- [ ] Hitting any limited endpoint past quota returns 429 with proper body.
- [ ] Headers `RateLimit-*` exposed for debugging.
- [ ] Limits configurable via env vars for tests.
- [ ] No regressions in existing UCs.

## Dependencies
None. Should land before M6 feature work where possible.
title: FB1-M7-S1: Rate limiting middleware on all POST/PATCH endpoints (express-rate-limit)

body:
# Customer Feedback Round 1 — Epic

**Spec:** [`docs/superpowers/specs/2026-05-24-customer-feedback-round-1-design.md`](../blob/main/docs/superpowers/specs/2026-05-24-customer-feedback-round-1-design.md)
**Customer meeting:** 2026-05-24
**Milestone:** M6 Customer Feedback R1 — due 2026-06-20
**Branch:** `feat/customer-feedback-round-1` (planned, off `integration/uc04-uc05-redesign`)

## Customer asks addressed (10 items, all Arabic)

1. زيد خيار الدركون عشان اذا معوش هوية → §4 No-ID option
2. الصلاحيات تبعت المتطوع، ومعلومات اكثر عن المتطوع عشان يسجل → §6 Volunteer signup
3. صفحة الادمن تقدر توافق على المتطوعين، برضو كل الطلبات تبعت الزباين، احصائيات، يكون عندها ليست لكلشي → §7
4. يقدر اليوزر يختار ديد لاين → §5.2 Deadline
5. خلي الايميل بصفخة الطلب اوتو لانو مسجل → §5.1 Auto-fill
6. صفحة الدايريكتوري لازمها تعديل، لازم اليست اتبين اول اشي فوق → §8
7. الادمن يقدر يعطي صلاحبة لناس ثانية انهم يكونو ادمن، وكمان يكون للادمن ويبسايت كامل → §7.1 + §7.5
8. الموقع فاضي لازمو صور واشياء ولازم يكون احلى → §9
9. اليوزر يقدر يقيم الفولنتير والعموتا كاملة → §10 Ratings
10. سجل او هيستوري للبقشوت → §11 Request timeline

## Work plan (18 child issues, dependency order)

### Phase A — Foundations (must merge first)
- [ ] #63 — Add `users/{uid}` profile collection + `/api/users/me` (@MuhammadMarmash)
- [ ] #64 — Schema additions to `requests` + rules deltas (@abdullahabulafi)
- [ ] #65 — `writeRequestEvent` helper + `requestEvents` collection + rules (@abdullahabulafi)

### Phase B — UC-01 request form (@MuhammadMarmash)
- [ ] #66 — \"No Israeli ID\" option (blocks: #64)
- [ ] #67 — Auto-fill from profile + email override + save-to-profile toast (blocks: #63)
- [ ] #68 — Optional deadline picker + My Requests timeline (blocks: #64, #65)

### Phase C — Volunteer signup (@mhammadsiag)
- [ ] #69 — `/register` two-tab toggle + step-2 form + thanks page (blocks: #63, #70)
- [ ] #70 — `POST /api/volunteers/apply` + `volunteerApplications` collection (blocks: #63)
- [ ] #71 — Chat-on-assign system message (blocks: #75)

### Phase D — Admin shell + sub-pages (@abdullahabulafi)
- [ ] #72 — Admin shell + sidebar + hide public nav inside `/admin/*`
- [ ] #73 — Admin Volunteers page + approve/reject/deactivate + `volunteers/{uid}` mirror (blocks: #70, #72)
- [ ] #74 — Admin Requests list + detail UI (blocks: #64, #65, #72, #75)
- [ ] #75 — Admin Requests endpoints: assign + status + note (blocks: #64, #65, #73)
- [ ] #76 — Admin Users page + promote/demote + deactivate + guards (blocks: #63, #72)
- [ ] #77 — Admin Dashboard stats (blocks: #72, #73, #75, #76, #80)

### Phase E — Directory + site polish (@hamzakaraky)
- [ ] #78 — Directory layout: list above the fold + collapsible filters
- [ ] #79 — Asset manifest + `<AssetImage>` + photo slots + section polish

### Phase F — Ratings (@MuhammadMarmash)
- [ ] #80 — Ratings endpoint + end-of-request rate form + volunteer aggregates (blocks: #63, #65, #73, #75)

## Risks (from spec §15)

1. Customer photo pack delivery — if not received by 2026-06-15, ship demo with CC0-stock fallback via the manifest.
2. Demo timeline — must phase so each phase is independently shippable, no half-broken intermediate states.
3. Volunteer claim refresh — on approve, `revokeRefreshTokens` forces re-auth; UI must handle 401 → \"sign in again\".
4. Last-admin demotion — server-side guard required.
5. Rating re-aggregation cost — fine at NGO scale; revisit if it grows.
6. `requestEvents` unbounded growth — fine at NGO scale.
7. HE/EN parity — every new string lands in both locales in the same PR.

## Definition of done
All checkboxes above ticked + spec §17 acceptance checklist passes in a live demo run on a clean Firebase project.
title: FB1: Customer feedback round 1 — epic

body:
## Context
Round-1 customer feedback. Spec: `docs/superpowers/specs/2026-05-24-customer-feedback-round-1-design.md` §10. Customer ask: \"اليوزر يقدر يقيم الفولنتير والعموتا كاملة\".

## Scope
**Data:** Add `ratings/{ratingId}` collection where `ratingId = requestId` (deterministic to prevent dupes). Shape per §10.3.

**Backend:** `POST /api/ratings { requestId, volunteerStars?, volunteerComment?, ngoStars, ngoComment? }`:
- Caller must be the request's beneficiary.
- `request.status === 'resolved'`.
- No existing `ratings/{requestId}` doc (one-shot per request).
- If `request.assignedVolunteerId` is set, `volunteerStars` is required.
- After write: recompute `avgVolunteerRating` and `totalRatings` on `volunteers/{volunteerId}` (read all ratings for that volunteer, take mean).
- Write a `rated` requestEvent.

**Frontend:**
- On `MyRequestsPage` detail, when `status === 'resolved'` and no rating yet, show a \"Rate your experience\" card at the top.
- Form: 1–5 stars + optional comment per dimension (volunteer + NGO). Volunteer block hidden if no volunteer was assigned.
- Stars: keyboard accessible (arrow keys, space to set).
- Submit disabled until required stars are set.

**Rules:** `ratings` read by owner/rated-volunteer/admin; write server-only.

## Acceptance criteria
- [ ] Beneficiary can rate exactly once per resolved request.
- [ ] Submitting twice for the same request returns 409.
- [ ] Volunteer's `avgVolunteerRating` is correct after each rating.
- [ ] A `rated` event appears in the timeline.
- [ ] HE+EN strings.

## Dependencies
Blocked by: #63 (users), #65 (events), #73 (volunteers collection), #75 (resolved status transitions).
title: FB1: Ratings — POST /api/ratings + end-of-request rate form + volunteer aggregates

body: ## Context Round-1 customer feedback. Spec: `docs/superpowers/specs/2026-05-24-customer-feedback-round-1-design.md` §9. Customer ask: \"الموقع فاضي لازمو صور واشياء ولازم يكون احلى\". Customer photos are a hard external dependency (see spec §15 Risk 1). This issue ships the placeholder infrastructure + slot wiring; photos drop in later without code changes. ## Scope - New file `frontend/src/assets/manifest.ts` per §9.1, listing every image slot with id, page, description, aspectRatio, altHe, altEn, state, src?, credit?. - New component `` that: - Renders a labeled gray placeholder (with optional skeleton shimmer) at the correct aspect ratio when state=`placeholder`. - Renders `` with explicit width/height when state≠`placeholder`. - Picks `altHe` or `altEn` based on current language. - Wire all 14 slots from §9.2 across HomePage, LoginPage, RegisterPage. - Polish bundled per §9.3: - Homepage vertical spacing 96px desktop / 64px mobile between sections. - Subtle cupped-hands SVG dividers between sections. ## Out of scope - The customer photos themselves (delivered later by the NGO). ## Acceptance criteria - [ ] All 14 slots render placeholders (no broken images, no layout shift). - [ ] Flipping a manifest entry from `placeholder` to `customer` with a filename in `public/assets/customer/` shows the photo with no other code change. - [ ] CLS score on HomePage stays in \"good\". - [ ] Pages no longer feel empty — confirmed by team review. ## Dependencies None.
title: FB1: Asset manifest + <AssetImage> component + photo slots on HomePage/Login/Register

body:
## Context
Round-1 customer feedback. Spec: `docs/superpowers/specs/2026-05-24-customer-feedback-round-1-design.md` §8. Customer ask: \"صفحة الدايريكتوري لازمها تعديل، لازم اليست اتبين اول اشي فوق قبل ما يقدر ينزل\".

## Scope
- Remove `PageHeader` from `DirectoryPage`.
- Replace with a 56px-tall slim bar:
  - Tab switcher (Businesses / Answers) on the start side.
  - Search input + \"Filters\" button on the end side.
- The list begins immediately under that bar.
- Filters move into a collapsible accordion panel above the list, collapsed by default.
- List grid stays `repeat(auto-fill, minmax(280px, 1fr))`.

## Acceptance criteria
- [ ] On 1366×768 viewport in both HE and EN, at least 3 list cards are at least partially visible without scrolling on first paint.
- [ ] Filter accordion expands/collapses smoothly.
- [ ] Existing search and filter logic still works.
- [ ] HE+EN strings.

## Dependencies
None.
title: FB1: Directory layout — slim header, list above the fold, collapsible filters

body:
## Context
Round-1 customer feedback. Spec: `docs/superpowers/specs/2026-05-24-customer-feedback-round-1-design.md` §7.4. Customer ask: \"احصائيات\".

## Scope
**Backend:** `GET /api/admin/stats` returns aggregate counters using Firestore `count()` queries (cheap, no doc reads):
- Pending approvals by type: businesses, organizations, answers, volunteers.
- Requests by status: submitted, assigned, in_progress, resolved, rejected.
- Users total + by role.
- Ratings: avg volunteer stars, avg NGO stars, total rated requests.
- Activity: requests submitted in last 7d / 30d.

**Frontend (`/admin` dashboard):**
- Card grid showing each of the above with clear labels.
- Empty states when count=0.
- Loading skeletons.

## Out of scope
- Drill-down charts; just numbers in cards for this round.

## Acceptance criteria
- [ ] `/api/admin/stats` returns a single JSON object with all numbers.
- [ ] Dashboard renders all cards even when some counts are zero.
- [ ] HE+EN strings.

## Dependencies
Blocked by: #72 (admin shell), #73 (volunteers), #75 (requests endpoints), #76 (users endpoints), #80 (ratings)
title: FB1: Admin Dashboard — /admin stats cards + GET /api/admin/stats

body:
## Context
Round-1 customer feedback. Spec: `docs/superpowers/specs/2026-05-24-customer-feedback-round-1-design.md` §7.5. Customer ask: \"الادمن لازم يكون عندو المقدرة يعطي صلاحبة لناس ثانية انهم يكونو ادمن\".

## Scope
**Frontend (`/admin/users`):**
- Table: email, displayName, role, createdAt, disabled flag. Filter by role, search by email or name. Pagination.
- Row actions:
  - Promote to admin (confirmation modal).
  - Demote from admin (only visible on admin rows; tooltip when disabled by guard).
  - Deactivate user (confirmation modal).

**Backend:**
- `GET /api/admin/users` — list with filters + cursor pagination.
- `POST /api/admin/users/promote { uid, role: 'admin'|'beneficiary' }`:
  - Server-side guards: cannot demote yourself (400); cannot demote the LAST remaining admin (400). Check by counting admins in users collection before allowing demote.
  - Set custom claim + mirror to `users/{uid}.role`.
  - AuditLog with before/after.
- `POST /api/admin/users/deactivate { uid, disabled: boolean }`:
  - Cannot deactivate yourself (400).
  - Set `users/{uid}.disabled`; call `firebaseAuth().updateUser(uid, { disabled })`.
  - AuditLog.

## Acceptance criteria
- [ ] Admin can promote a beneficiary to admin and demote them back.
- [ ] Attempting to demote yourself returns 400 with clear error.
- [ ] Attempting to demote the last admin returns 400 with clear error.
- [ ] Attempting to deactivate yourself returns 400.
- [ ] Every action writes an auditLog.
- [ ] HE+EN strings.

## Dependencies
Blocked by: #63 (users collection), #72 (admin shell)
title: FB1: Admin Users page — list + promote/demote + deactivate, with last-admin and self-demote guards

body:
## Context
Round-1 customer feedback. Spec: `docs/superpowers/specs/2026-05-24-customer-feedback-round-1-design.md` §7.3 (backend). Customer ask: \"تقدر تربط الفولنتير مع الزباين\".

## Scope
- `GET /api/admin/requests` — list with filters (status, category, urgency, hasDeadline, assignedOrNot, beneficiarySearch) + cursor pagination.
- `GET /api/admin/requests/:id` — single request detail.
- `POST /api/admin/requests/assign { requestId, volunteerId }`:
  - Set `requests/{id}.assignedVolunteerId`, `assignedAt`, status=`assigned`.
  - Increment `volunteers/{volunteerId}.activeAssignments`.
  - Write `requestEvents` entry: type=`assigned`, payload={volunteerId, volunteerName}.
  - Write auditLog.
  - Trigger chat integration #71 (separate issue, this one just emits the event).
- `POST /api/admin/requests/status { requestId, to, note? }`:
  - Allowed transitions only (submitted→assigned→in_progress→resolved/rejected). Reject backward transitions with 400.
  - On `resolved`: decrement `activeAssignments` on the volunteer.
  - Write `status_changed` event + auditLog.
- `POST /api/admin/requests/note { requestId, body }`:
  - Write `note_added` event with `visibility='staff'` + auditLog.

## Out of scope
- Frontend UI for these actions (covered by #12).
- Chat system message (#71).

## Acceptance criteria
- [ ] All five endpoints exist and require admin role.
- [ ] Each mutating endpoint writes both `requestEvents` (via writeRequestEvent) and `auditLogs`.
- [ ] Status transitions are enforced; invalid transitions return 400.
- [ ] `activeAssignments` counter stays consistent across assign/resolve.

## Dependencies
Blocked by: #64 (schema), #65 (events helper), #73 (volunteers collection)
title: FB1: Admin Requests — assignment + status change + internal note endpoints

body:
## Context
Round-1 customer feedback. Spec: `docs/superpowers/specs/2026-05-24-customer-feedback-round-1-design.md` §7.3 (list + detail UI). Customer ask: \"كل الطلبات تبعت الزباين، يكون عندها ليست لكلشي\".

## Scope
**List view (`/admin/requests`):**
- Filterable table: status, category, urgency, has-deadline, assigned-or-not, beneficiary search.
- Columns: short ID, beneficiary, category, urgency, status, deadline, assigned volunteer, submitted at.
- \"no ID\" badge on rows where `idType !== 'israeli'`.
- Deadline column sortable.
- Row link to detail view.
- Backend list endpoint exists in #75 — `GET /api/admin/requests` with filters and cursor pagination.

**Detail view (`/admin/requests/[id]`):**
- Full request fields (read-only).
- Attachments with signed-URL download links.
- Timeline rendering using `requestEvents` from #65 — full timeline including `visibility='staff'` notes labeled \"staff only\".
- Admin actions UI: Assign volunteer modal, Status change dropdown, Add internal note textarea — call endpoints from #75.

## Out of scope
- The mutating endpoints themselves (covered by #75).
- Beneficiary-side timeline (covered by #68).

## Acceptance criteria
- [ ] Admin can see all requests with working filters and pagination.
- [ ] Detail page shows complete request + full timeline + admin action UI.
- [ ] Assign modal lists only volunteers whose `areas` intersect the request category, sorted by activeAssignments asc.
- [ ] HE+EN strings.

## Dependencies
Blocked by: #64, #65, #72, #75
title: FB1: Admin Requests list + detail page (/admin/requests, /admin/requests/[id])

body:
## Context
Round-1 customer feedback. Spec: `docs/superpowers/specs/2026-05-24-customer-feedback-round-1-design.md` §6.4 + §7.2. Customer ask: \"الادمن تقدر توافق على المتطوعين\".

## Scope
**Backend:**
- Add `volunteers/{uid}` collection (denormalized) per §6.4. Public read; server-only write.
- Endpoints:
  - `GET /api/admin/volunteers/pending` — list pending applications.
  - `POST /api/admin/volunteers/approve { appId }` — set `users/{uid}.role='volunteer'`, set Auth custom claim via Admin SDK, call `revokeRefreshTokens(uid)`, copy fields into `volunteers/{uid}` (active=true), update application status to approved with reviewedBy/reviewedAt, write auditLog.
  - `POST /api/admin/volunteers/reject { appId, note }` — set application rejected with note, audit-log. Does NOT delete the user.
  - `GET /api/admin/volunteers/active`.
  - `POST /api/admin/volunteers/deactivate { uid }` — set `volunteers/{uid}.active=false`, demote claim back to beneficiary, audit-log.

**Frontend (`/admin/volunteers`):**
- Two stacked sections: Pending applications + Active volunteers.
- Pending row: name, submitted date, areas chips, city. Actions: Approve / Reject (modal with required note).
- Active row: name, areas, city, activeAssignments count, avg rating. Actions: View profile modal, Deactivate (modal confirm).

## Out of scope
- Avg rating displayed here comes from #18; until then show \"—\".

## Acceptance criteria
- [ ] Approve flow promotes the user to volunteer and creates `volunteers/{uid}`.
- [ ] Reject flow stores note and leaves user as beneficiary.
- [ ] Deactivate flow demotes claim and flips `active=false`.
- [ ] All four endpoints write auditLogs entries.
- [ ] Firestore rules updated for `volunteers/{uid}` (public read, server write).
- [ ] HE+EN strings.

## Dependencies
Blocked by: #70 (apply endpoint), #72 (admin shell)
title: FB1: Admin Volunteers page — pending+active tables, approve/reject/deactivate, volunteers/{uid} mirror

body:
## Context
Round-1 customer feedback. Spec: `docs/superpowers/specs/2026-05-24-customer-feedback-round-1-design.md` §7.1. Customer ask: \"يكون للادمن ويبسايت كامل مش صفحة\".

Phase-D foundation — blocks #11, #12, #14, #15.

## Scope
- Convert current single `/admin` page into a route subtree:
  - `/admin` → Dashboard (placeholder for #15)
  - `/admin/approvals` → today's queue (move current `AdminPage.jsx` content here)
  - `/admin/volunteers` → placeholder (#11 fills it)
  - `/admin/requests` → placeholder (#12 fills it)
  - `/admin/users` → placeholder (#14 fills it)
  - `/admin/settings` → placeholder
- Layout component that wraps every `/admin/*` page:
  - Top bar (brand + Admin eyebrow + signed-in user + sign-out).
  - Left sidebar (desktop) / top scroll tabs (mobile) with the 6 sections.
  - Hides the public `Navbar` and `Footer` when `router.pathname.startsWith('/admin')`.
- Auth gate inside the layout: if `role !== 'admin'` redirect to `/` with toast (backend already enforced via `requireRole('admin')`).

## Out of scope
- Stats cards on dashboard (#15).
- Sub-page contents (#11, #12, #14).

## Acceptance criteria
- [ ] Visiting `/admin/anything` while signed out redirects to `/login`.
- [ ] Visiting `/admin` as non-admin redirects to `/`.
- [ ] As admin, the public Navbar/Footer are not visible anywhere under `/admin/*`.
- [ ] Existing approvals queue functionality intact at `/admin/approvals`.
- [ ] HE+EN strings for sidebar items.

## Dependencies
None. Blocks: #11, #12, #14, #15.
title: FB1: Admin shell — convert /admin to route subtree with sidebar + hide public nav inside /admin/*

body:
## Context
Round-1 customer feedback. Spec: `docs/superpowers/specs/2026-05-24-customer-feedback-round-1-design.md` §7.3.

When admin assigns a volunteer to a request (endpoint added in #75), a chat (UC-04) should be created or joined with participants = [beneficiaryId, volunteerId, adminId], and a system message posted: \"המתנדב/ת שובץ/ה / Volunteer has been assigned\".

## Scope
- Extend the assignment handler from #75 to also call into the chat service:
  - Create a chat if none exists for this request, or join the existing one (chat doc shape per UC-04 — see existing `chats` collection).
  - Add a participants update so the assigned volunteer is included.
  - Post a system message (`messages` collection write) attributed to a synthetic `system` actor or to the admin user (decide based on existing message schema).
- Both HE and EN system-message strings, picked at write-time based on the beneficiary's preferred language (fall back to HE).

## Out of scope
- Any new chat features — must reuse the existing UC-04 schemas.
- Frontend changes to the chat UI.

## Acceptance criteria
- [ ] Assigning a volunteer creates/updates the chat with all three participants.
- [ ] A system message appears in the chat history immediately after assignment.
- [ ] No regressions to existing UC-04 chat behavior.

## Dependencies
Blocked by: #75
title: FB1: Chat-on-assign integration — system message into the request chat when admin assigns volunteer

body:
## Context
Round-1 customer feedback. Spec: `docs/superpowers/specs/2026-05-24-customer-feedback-round-1-design.md` §6.3.

## Scope
- Add Firestore collection `volunteerApplications/{appId}` with shape from §6.3.
- Add route `POST /api/volunteers/apply` — caller must be authenticated; validates body via Zod; creates the application doc with `status='pending'`, `submittedAt=now`, and also ensures `users/{uid}` profile exists (lazy-create via the helper from #63).
- Firestore rules: `volunteerApplications/{appId}` read by owner OR admin; write server-only.
- Reject if the caller already has a pending application (one-active-application rule).

## Out of scope
- Approval/reject endpoints (covered by #11).
- Frontend UI (covered by #7).

## Acceptance criteria
- [ ] `POST /api/volunteers/apply` returns 201 on success and 409 if a pending application exists.
- [ ] Zod schema enforces min/max lengths and required arrays from §6.1.
- [ ] Firestore rules-unit-tests cover owner read, non-owner denied, admin read, client write denied.
- [ ] AuditLog entry written.

## Dependencies
Blocked by: #63
title: FB1: Volunteer application backend — POST /api/volunteers/apply + volunteerApplications collection + rules

body:
## Context
Round-1 customer feedback. Spec: `docs/superpowers/specs/2026-05-24-customer-feedback-round-1-design.md` §6.1. Customer ask: \"الصلاحيات تبعت المتطوع، ومعلومات اكثر عن المتطوع عشان يسجل\".

## Scope
- `/register` gets a two-tab toggle at top: \"אני נעזר / I need help\" (beneficiary, default) ↔ \"אני מתנדב / I want to volunteer\" (volunteer).
- Volunteer flow: step 1 = email/password (same as today), step 2 = volunteer details form per §6.1:
  - firstName, lastName (1..80)
  - phone (IL phone regex)
  - city (1..80)
  - areas chips (multi, min 1, from 4 categories)
  - languages chips (multi, min 1, from he/ar/en/am/ti)
  - availability chips (multi, min 1, from weekdays/evenings/weekends/flexible)
  - motivation textarea (30..600 chars)
- On submit: create Firebase Auth user → `POST /api/auth/register` (sets beneficiary claim) → `POST /api/volunteers/apply` (#8, creates application).
- New page `/register/volunteer/thanks`: \"תודה — אנחנו נבדוק / Thanks — we'll review your application.\"
- User stays as beneficiary in the meantime so they can use the site.

## Out of scope
- Backend application creation (covered by #8).
- Admin approval UI (covered by #11).

## Acceptance criteria
- [ ] New user can register as volunteer end-to-end and lands on thanks page.
- [ ] Validation errors appear inline before any API call.
- [ ] HE+EN strings for all new copy in LanguageContext.
- [ ] Beneficiary flow unchanged.

## Dependencies
Blocked by: #63 (#8 will be filed next and is also a dependency)
title: FB1: Volunteer signup — /register two-tab toggle + step-2 form + thanks page

body:
## Context
Round-1 customer feedback. Spec: `docs/superpowers/specs/2026-05-24-customer-feedback-round-1-design.md` §5.2. Customer ask: \"يقدر اليوزر يختار ديد لاين\".

## Scope
- Step 2 of `RequestsPage` gets an optional native `<input type=\"date\">` labeled \"תאריך יעד / Deadline\".
- Validation: must be ≥ today, ≤ today+365d, empty allowed.
- Send `deadline` in the `POST /api/requests` payload (backend schema work done in #64).
- `MyRequestsPage` row: show a \"Due in X days\" pill when `deadline` is set; pill turns ember when X ≤ 3.
- Also wire timeline rendering on My Requests detail page (the beneficiary view) using `requestEvents` from #65 — only events where `visibility='all'`.

## Out of scope
- Volunteer \"my assignments\" view (deferred per spec §5.2).
- Admin requests list deadline column (covered by #12).

## Acceptance criteria
- [ ] Past or >365d future dates are rejected with a field error.
- [ ] Empty deadline submits normally.
- [ ] My Requests row shows the Due-in-X pill with correct color.
- [ ] My Requests detail page shows a human-readable timeline (only public events).
- [ ] HE+EN strings in LanguageContext.

## Dependencies
Blocked by: #64, #65
title: FB1: Request form — optional deadline picker + My Requests Due-in-X pill

body:
## Context
Round-1 customer feedback. Spec: `docs/superpowers/specs/2026-05-24-customer-feedback-round-1-design.md` §5.1. Customer ask: \"خلي الايميل بصفخة الطلب اوتو لانو مسجل\".

## Scope
- On `/requests` mount, call `GET /api/users/me` and pre-populate `firstName`, `lastName`, `email`, `phone`, `city` from the response.
- Missing profile fields stay empty.
- All five fields remain editable. Email defaults to the user's Auth email but can be overridden for this request.
- Overriding email does NOT change the auth email; it only affects this submission.
- After successful submit, if any profile-derived field was edited, show a non-blocking toast: \"Save these details to your profile?\" with two buttons (Save / Not now). Save calls `PATCH /api/users/me`.

## Out of scope
- A dedicated profile-edit page.

## Acceptance criteria
- [ ] First-time user with no profile sees empty fields and can submit normally.
- [ ] Returning user sees their profile pre-filled and can submit without typing anything new.
- [ ] Email override on one request does not change the user's auth email.
- [ ] Save-to-profile toast appears only when a profile-derived field was edited.
- [ ] HE+EN strings in LanguageContext.

## Dependencies
Blocked by: #63
title: FB1: Request form — auto-fill from profile + email override + save-to-profile toast

body:
## Context
Round-1 customer feedback. Spec: `docs/superpowers/specs/2026-05-24-customer-feedback-round-1-design.md` §4. Customer ask: \"زيد خيار الدركون عشان اذا معوش هوية\".

## Scope
- On step 1 of `RequestsPage`, below the ID number field, add checkbox \"אין לי תעודת זהות ישראלית / I don't have an Israeli ID\".
- When checked: disable + clear the ID input; show \"ID type\" select (`passport` / `in_process` / `other`) and optional \"פירוט / Notes\" textarea (max 200).
- When unchecked: `idType` resets to `israeli`, ID input required as today.
- Send `idType` and `idNote` in the `POST /api/requests` payload.
- Backend schema work already covered by #64 — confirm the route accepts the new fields.

## Out of scope
- Admin badge for \"no ID\" requests (covered by #12 in the admin requests list).

## Acceptance criteria
- [ ] User can submit a request without an Israeli ID number.
- [ ] Backend rejects `idType='israeli'` + missing `idNumber` with a field error.
- [ ] Backend rejects `idType!='israeli'` + non-empty `idNumber` with a field error.
- [ ] HE+EN strings in LanguageContext.
- [ ] No regression to existing UC-01 happy path.

## Dependencies
Blocked by: #64
title: FB1: Request form — "No Israeli ID" option

body:
## Context
Round-1 customer feedback. Spec: `docs/superpowers/specs/2026-05-24-customer-feedback-round-1-design.md` §11.

Phase-A foundation — the per-request timeline backbone. Every mutating request endpoint calls this helper. Must land before #12, #13, #18.

## Scope
- Add `requestEvents/{eventId}` collection with shape from §11.1: `requestId`, `type`, `actorId`, `actorRole`, `payload`, `visibility ('all'|'staff')`, `createdAt`.
- Event types: `created`, `assigned`, `status_changed`, `note_added`, `rated`, `attachment_added`.
- Add shared helper `writeRequestEvent({ requestId, type, actorId, actorRole, payload?, visibility? })` in `backend/src/lib/`.
- Wire `POST /api/requests` (existing) to write a `created` event so the timeline isn't empty for new requests.
- Wire existing upload-completion path (UC-01-b) to write `attachment_added` events.
- Firestore rules: read by parent request beneficiary (if `visibility='all'`) OR assigned volunteer OR admin; write server-only.

## Out of scope
- Admin-side mutation endpoints that write events (covered by #13).
- The two consumer UIs — beneficiary timeline (#6 or new sub-issue) and admin detail timeline (#12).
- Rating event (covered by #18).

## Acceptance criteria
- [ ] `writeRequestEvent` helper exists and is type-safe (Zod or TS-only).
- [ ] Submitting a request now writes a `created` event.
- [ ] Uploading an attachment writes an `attachment_added` event.
- [ ] Firestore rules updated and rules-unit-test confirms staff-visibility events are hidden from beneficiary.

## Dependencies
None. Blocks: #12, #13, #18.
title: FB1: Shared writeRequestEvent() helper + requestEvents collection + rules

body:
## Context
Round-1 customer feedback. Spec: `docs/superpowers/specs/2026-05-24-customer-feedback-round-1-design.md` §4, §5.2, §7.3, §12.

Phase-A foundation — every other request-related issue writes against this shape. Must merge before #4, #6, #12, #13.

## Scope
`requests` collection field additions / changes:
- `idNumber` → optional (currently required)
- Add `idType: 'israeli'|'passport'|'in_process'|'other'` default `'israeli'`
- Add `idNote: string|null` (max 200)
- Add `deadline: Timestamp|null` (≥ today, ≤ today+365d)
- Add `assignedVolunteerId: string|null`
- Add `assignedAt: Timestamp|null`
- Document the official status enum: `'submitted'|'assigned'|'in_progress'|'resolved'|'rejected'`

Update the Zod schema in `backend/src/routes/requests.ts` to accept the new fields with the cross-field rule for `idType === 'israeli' ↔ idNumber required`.

Firestore rules — `requests/{requestId}`:
- Add read for `assignedVolunteerId == request.auth.uid` (assigned volunteer can read the request).
- Keep all writes server-only.

## Out of scope
- Frontend UI for the new fields (covered by #4, #6).
- Assignment endpoint (covered by #13).

## Acceptance criteria
- [ ] Zod schema accepts the new fields and rejects invalid combinations with 400.
- [ ] Existing `POST /api/requests` still works for the today-default case (idType israeli, no deadline).
- [ ] Firestore rules updated and rules-unit-test confirms assigned volunteer can read their request.
- [ ] No regressions to UC-01 main flow.

## Dependencies
None. Blocks: #4, #6, #12, #13.
title: FB1: Schema additions to requests + Firestore rules deltas

body:
## Context
Round-1 customer feedback. Spec: `docs/superpowers/specs/2026-05-24-customer-feedback-round-1-design.md` §6.2.

This is a Phase-A foundation — auto-fill (§5.1), volunteer signup (§6), and the admin users page (§7.5) all read from `users/{uid}`. Nothing else can start until this lands.

## Scope
- Add Firestore collection `users/{uid}` with shape defined in spec §6.2 (uid, email, firstName?, lastName?, phone?, city?, role, disabled, createdAt, updatedAt).
- Lazy-create the doc on `/api/auth/register` and on first `GET /api/users/me` if absent.
- Add endpoint `GET /api/users/me` — returns caller's profile (lazy-create).
- Add endpoint `PATCH /api/users/me` — updates `firstName`, `lastName`, `phone`, `city` only. Not email, not role.
- Firestore rules: read by owner OR admin; write server-only.
- Mirror the role custom-claim into `users/{uid}.role` on every claim change (called from existing register, future volunteer-approve, future promote).

## Out of scope
- Profile UI page (the auto-fill issue #5 uses `GET /api/users/me` directly).
- Avatar/photo upload.

## Acceptance criteria
- [ ] `GET /api/users/me` returns 200 for any signed-in user and creates the doc if missing.
- [ ] `PATCH /api/users/me` validates input via Zod, rejects email/role mutations with 400.
- [ ] Firestore rules updated and rules-unit-tests cover: owner read OK, non-owner read denied, client write denied.
- [ ] HE+EN strings in LanguageContext if any UI is exposed.
- [ ] No regressions in existing auth flow.

## Dependencies
None. This unblocks #5, #7, #8, #11, #14, #18.
title: FB1: Add users/{uid} profile collection + GET/PATCH /api/users/me

body:
﻿New wiki page Demo-Walkthrough.md with step-by-step demo flow:
1. Register beneficiary
2. Submit request
3. Admin approves a business
4. Beneficiary chats with assigned volunteer

Include at least 6 screenshots at each major step. Both HE and EN screenshots.

Acceptance: page is published; walks the prof + customer through the full app in 10 minutes of reading.
title: P-4: Demo walkthrough page + screenshots in wiki

- Backend deploys to Cloud Run or Vercel - decide in Week 5 standup.
- Set up the staging URL.

Acceptance: a new commit to main results in an updated public URL within 5 minutes.
title: P-3: Deploy to Firebase Hosting (frontend) + decide backend deploy

Acceptance: 1-paragraph alignment-check report posted in chat with "LGTM" from all 4 teammates.
title: P-2: Wiki doc-alignment second pass before demo
